author: jenpaff
content: Regarding the Hegotá headliner discussion: We’re not seeing clear user impact in the current EL headliner proposals and instead propose to focus the EL on clearer strategic work e.g. L1-zkEVM pathing, further improve state growth / execution performance . Any takes on this?

author: tersec
content:
Sure, I have a take, that "clear user impact" is a rhetorical proxy for whatever the person proposing it thinks is important, and has relatively little intrinsic semantic content. It's at best subjective to say the current EIPs don't provide "clear user impact" then propose L1-zkEVM, which are pretty indirect (useful, sure, but indirect) paths to improvements.

For example, to get their proving them they benefit from ePBS, which still hasn't had its first devnet and the EF is still making noises about, hm, will it actually end up shipping in Glamsterdam. Otherwise for all the ZK proving people talk about what they can prove in one slot duration (12s, or at some point 6s), no, they actually have something like 1s to 3s

author: tersec
content: tl;dr it's not so much about preferences around the existing headliner candidates or L1-zkEVM but "clear user impact" is an arbitrarily subjective and gameable metric

author: robocopsgonemad
content: i do like the idea of "give the users nothing" this hardfork. thats new. It's actually a pretty good argument for SSZ though, which pays off a ton of techdebt that users likely won't see.
